item.title: [02] Vanilla Emacs with Prot: I’ll Repent for My Markdown Sins
item.description:
youtube

In the previous coaching session with Prot we learned how to install emacs and how to start editing files and modifying our configuration from within emacs, we also installed our first packages. We'll continue installing a few more packages this time I guess and we'll get started with Org or orgmode Previous video can be found in the podcast channel: https://youtu.be/btAOBkcLEkg I wrote a bit more about it in a blogpost: https://linkarzu.com/posts/emacs/prot-love-letter/ Prot responded in his blogpost: https://protesilaos.com/codelog/2026-07-04-emacs-for-beginners-with-linkarzu/ If you want to learn more about Prot’s coaching sessions, you can visit this link: https://protesilaos.com/coach/ Special thanks to Sasha Chua for being there guiding me in my livestreams and taking notes to make the process easier, she also created an article and reacted to one of my videos: https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/07/re-react-to-sacha-and-prot-newbies-and-starter-kits-emacs-video-linkarzu/ If you have any of the questions below: * Why is the stream vertical, I cannot see a thing * Why are there 2 streams? * What’s that weird voice when someone sends a message * How can I send your GOATED emojis? * Why does TTS not work? * Why aren’t you reading chats on stream? * What’s that Ko-Fi progress bar we see in your videos? * How can I join your discord? * How can I share my setup and workflow in your channel? * Why did the livestream become members only after recording? Read this article: https://linkarzu.com/posts/news/streaming-info/ Support me: Buy me the Switch 2: https://ko-fi.com/linkarzu Join the YouTube Members: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join My Merch Store: https://merch.linkarzu.com My Dotfiles: https://github.com/linkarzu/dotfiles-latest Follow me on X: https://x.com/link_arzu

item.title: History of Emacs the Text Editor/Operative System
item.description:
youtube

00:00 The joke that explains everything 01:07 MIT AI Lab, 1976 — where it all began 02:33 The Social Contract — sharing before the GPL 03:03 The proliferation — when Emacs became an idea 03:34 James Gosling and the door that closed 04:13 GNU Emacs — a text editor that changed the world 04:57 Emacs Lisp — when using and writing become the same thing 06:31 The Editor War — Emacs vs Vi 07:52 The Schism — XEmacs and the cost of division 09:20 The dark years — Eclipse, IntelliJ, VSCode 10:16 The renaissance — Doom Emacs, Evil Mode, a new generation 11:02 Org-mode and Magit — why serious developers never left 12:52 What Emacs really is — an argument that never ended Emacs is older than Linux, older than the internet, and older than most of the people who use it. It started as a collection of macros at the MIT AI Lab in 1976, became the first program of the GNU Project, gave birth to the GPL, survived a religious schism, was written off as obsolete, and came back stronger. This is the full history of the text editor that refused to die — and why it still matters in 2025.

item.title: Emacs: global keybinding overrides
item.description:
youtube

In this video I demonstrate how to define key bindings that have a global overriding effect so that major modes cannot change them. This is important if you want to redefine keys that are not meant to be configured by the user. Find the init file I worked with on my website: https://protesilaos.com/codelog/2026-07-08-emacs-global-keybinding-overrides/

item.title: HyWiki: Zero Markup Hypertext
item.description:
youtube

This video is a tour of Hyperbole's HyWiki, a second brain system in Emacs that is unique for it's zero markup syntax and the omniscience of wiki pages within Emacs. Check out the blog post! https://www.chiply.dev/post-hyperbole-hywiki Timestamps: 0:00 Intro 1:24 2 Key Features 3:35 Omniscient Second Brain 4:29 Creating HyWiki Pages 6:58 HyWikiWords Appear Everywhere 10:50 Autocompletion 11:40 Subsections 14:10 Bind Any Action 17:58 Extensibility 22:05 Outro

item.title: [CLIP] Learning Vanilla Emacs from Scratch with Prot as a Neovim User
item.description:
youtube

This is a clip of the full interview I did with Prot yesterday, the link to the full video is here: https://youtu.be/btAOBkcLEkg I've been trying to try Emacs for the past few days on livestreams, I installed it but not sure if I installed the right version, then I started with the emacs manual, didn't finish it and just learned the basic things. And all this time I've wondered: Should I go evil mode and doom emacs, or should I go the vanilla emacs way? Which packages should I install, should I use a starter kit, should I even try emacs to begin with? So I reached out to Prot and he offered to do these sessions for free, I didn't accept his offering, so well pay him for 2 hours of his time every week and learn emacs. ------------------------------------------- WHERE ARE THE REST OF THE VIDEOS IN THE SERIES? For people that are confused and don't know what's going on, the edited video will be released in the podcast channel @linkarzu-podcast and it will be public and free for everyone The rest of the videos for the coaching sessions will be in the @linkarzu channel in a playlist called "The Church of Prot" ------------------------------------------- I wrote a bit more about it in a blogpost: https://linkarzu.com/posts/emacs/prot-love-letter/ Prot responded in his blogpost: https://protesilaos.com/codelog/2026-07-04-emacs-for-beginners-with-linkarzu/ If you want to learn more about Prot’s coaching sessions, you can visit this link: https://protesilaos.com/coach/ Special thanks to Sacha Chua for being there guiding me in my livestreams and taking notes to make the process easier, she also created an article and reacted to one of my videos: https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/07/re-react-to-sacha-and-prot-newbies-and-starter-kits-emacs-video-linkarzu/ Support me: Buy me the Switch 2: https://ko-fi.com/linkarzu Join the YouTube Members: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join My Merch Store: https://merch.linkarzu.com My Dotfiles: https://github.com/linkarzu/dotfiles-latest Follow me on X: https://x.com/link_arzu

item.title: Learning Vanilla Emacs from Scratch with Prot as a Neovim User
item.description:
youtube

I've been trying to try Emacs for the past few days on livestreams, I installed it but not sure if I installed the right version, then I started with the emacs manual, didn't finish it and just learned the basic things. And all this time I've wondered: Should I go evil mode and doom emacs, or should I go the vanilla emacs way? Which packages should I install, should I use a starter kit, should I even try emacs to begin with? So I reached out to Prot and he offered to do these sessions for free, I didn't accept his offering, so well pay him for 2 hours of his time every week and learn emacs. ------------------------------------------- WHERE ARE THE REST OF THE VIDEOS IN THE SERIES? For people that are confused and don't know what's going on, the edited video will be released in the podcast channel @linkarzu-podcast and it will be public and free for everyone The rest of the videos for the coaching sessions will be in the @linkarzu channel in a playlist called "The Church of Prot" ------------------------------------------- I wrote a bit more about it in a blogpost: https://linkarzu.com/posts/emacs/prot-love-letter/ Prot responded in his blogpost: https://protesilaos.com/codelog/2026-07-04-emacs-for-beginners-with-linkarzu/ If you want to learn more about Prot’s coaching sessions, you can visit this link: https://protesilaos.com/coach/ Special thanks to Sacha Chua for being there guiding me in my livestreams and taking notes to make the process easier, she also created an article and reacted to one of my videos: https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/07/re-react-to-sacha-and-prot-newbies-and-starter-kits-emacs-video-linkarzu/ Support me: Buy me the Switch 2: https://ko-fi.com/linkarzu Join the YouTube Members: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join My Merch Store: https://merch.linkarzu.com My Dotfiles: https://github.com/linkarzu/dotfiles-latest Follow me on X: https://x.com/link_arzu 00:00:00 Highlights 00:02:25 What do you do for a living and what are the coaching sessions about? 00:07:06 How does it feel to work for yourself and not depend on a company? 00:09:46 Why cool hairstyles and looks every week? 00:11:17 Do you make significant money out of YouTube? 00:15:51 What is emacs-plus on macos? 00:16:50 What is the --with-native-comp install flag and is it needed? 00:17:58 How to install emacs as a macos user and why emacs 30? 00:19:52 Emacs --fg-daemon, what is it and why? 00:21:24 I deleted the .emacs.d directory and also created a symlink 00:25:25 Doom emacs, evil mode or vanilla emacs? 00:29:21 Why is Linkarzu trying emacs? 00:32:02 menu-bar scroll-bar visible-bell lexical-binding 00:34:52 How to disable the yellow annoying warning on Mac 00:39:30 Should I finish the emacs tutorial as a beginner? 00:42:17 Why do you use Home, PgDn, PgUp, End 00:45:35 macOS supports emacs keybindings by default 00:50:56 Why you use the arrows to navigate in emacs? 00:53:50 Install some packages, vertico, marginalia, orderless 01:04:58 What do you think of AI for learning emacs? 01:10:14 Install marginalia 01:14:40 M-x marginalia-mode to toggle marginalia 01:15:57 Modify savehist 01:18:15 What is orderless and how to install it 01:26:32 Thoughts on homerow mods, one shot modifiers 01:29:42 Bob gigalord kofi donation. What to talk in coaching sessions. Read superchats 01:33:37 Let's continue with orderless 01:36:40 custom-file, export some config to another file and read from it 01:44:24 How to select text in emacs and configure delsel 01:48:35 Homework for next week, emacs tutorial, create an org file 01:50:58 This first video will be in the podcast channel, the rest in the normal channel 01:53:16 We'll see you live next week.

item.title: How to Search For Text in Emacs
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to search for text in Emacs with I-search, using the command: C-s

item.title: Emacs-LinkXall Hyperlinking In Program Source Files
item.description:
youtube

This shows how LinkXall supports hyperlinking between text files in Emacs or LibreOffice and documents in LibreOffice. "LinkXall Automates Hyperlinking Of Personal and Professional Information" explains how to get LinkXall from GitHub

item.title: Browse GitHub repos directly from find-file dialog
item.description:
youtube

Demo of "El Remoto" package as shown in Emacs ATX July 2026 meetup. https://github.com/agzam/remoto.el Complete meetup video is here: https://youtu.be/ZRoT-3yAMUQ

item.title: Seamless view/editing of markdown as org-mode
item.description:
youtube

Demo of an experimental package "El Prisma" as shown in Emacs ATX July 2026 meetup. https://github.com/agzam/prisma.el Complete meetup video is here: https://youtu.be/ZRoT-3yAMUQ

item.title: Emacs ATX meetup. July 2026. pi; gptel; completion-preview; prisma; remoto.
item.description:
youtube

0:00 - Intro 1:07 - Local LLM coding assistant: pi + Qwen3 on a GTX 1080 7:42 - Fixing a Projectile project-root bug 10:49 - Multi-cursor search & replace with Embark 12:15 - Screen tearing & the Lucid toolkit 18:00 - Awesome gptel improvements: an overlay interface 38:33 - Agentic edits & including AGENTS.md 41:00 - GPTel internals: session routing & point targets 53:04 - Org-roam, Org Agenda & Org Edna 1:03:01 - Completion: Corfu & completion-preview-mode 1:19:00 - Editing Markdown, YAML & JSON as Org 1:37:01 - Remoto: browse GitHub repos as files 1:52:06 - How Remoto fetches without shelling out 2:06:01 - Closing thoughts

item.title: Neovim user Reacts to Bashbunni's Doom Emacs Video
item.description:
youtube

I've been trying emacs for a few days on Livestream, but I'm more lost than ever, should I stick to Vanilla Emacs, or as a Neovim user should I just go with Evil Mode and Doom Emacs? I want to try the defaults, but am I doing the wrong thing? Can this video help us a little bit as I think Bashbunni was a Neovim user in the past Full livestream where this comes out of: https://youtube.com/live/qX_0ZlGnRhI Instructions to join the discord server https://discord.gg/D7CZMzw3rM Members: If you want your message read out loud, include !say Example: !say I have a small pp Donations of $10 or more on Ko-fi, preferred, or YouTube Super Chats will show up on screen and be read aloud by a computer voice. Support me: Buy me the Switch 2: https://ko-fi.com/linkarzu Join the YouTube Members (gives you access to the private discord server) https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join My Merch Store: https://merch.linkarzu.com My Dotfiles: https://github.com/linkarzu/dotfiles-latest Follow me on X: https://x.com/link_arzu

item.title: 3 Neovim Users Talk About Emacs, Which One is Better?
item.description:
youtube

Had a livestream yesterday in which 2 Discord members joined me to share about their own workflow. Both of them are Neovim ex-users, 1 of them fully switched to Emacs. We talk about both Emacs and Neovim and why they prefer each Full livestream where this comes out of: https://youtube.com/live/qX_0ZlGnRhI Instructions to join the discord server https://discord.gg/D7CZMzw3rM Members: If you want your message read out loud, include !say Example: !say I have a small pp Donations of $10 or more on Ko-fi, preferred, or YouTube Super Chats will show up on screen and be read aloud by a computer voice. Support me: Buy me the Switch 2: https://ko-fi.com/linkarzu Join the YouTube Members (gives you access to the private discord server) https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join My Merch Store: https://merch.linkarzu.com My Dotfiles: https://github.com/linkarzu/dotfiles-latest Follow me on X: https://x.com/link_arzu

item.title: Emacs: write with input method (e.g. French) and Jinx for spelling
item.description:
youtube

In this video I demonstrate how I use Emacs to write prose in French. I rely on the built-in framework for input methods to compose characters. I also use the Jinx package to do spell-checking. Here is the configuration I show in the video: https://protesilaos.com/codelog/2026-07-02-emacs-french-input-method-jinx-spell/

item.title: Emacs | God mode notes with denote and consult notes
item.description:
youtube

Consult notes is a package that hooks into your note-taking system. It works particularly well with Denote, picking up your notes directory variable automatically. Blog post: https://chrismaiorana.com/god-mode-notes/ My interest in having a note system comes down to whether it helps my writing. A writer needs different kinds of notes: reading notes, notes on craft, notes on etymology, and random ideas. Check out my books: https://chrismaiorana.com/books/

item.title: Learning Emacs with The Professor from University of Victoria
item.description:
youtube

The professor sent me an email one day explaining how emacs is a way of life and I found it intriguing as it was well written and made sense. He seems to be an emacs enthusiast and also seems to be using macOS, so I want to know more about the way he uses his workflow. So far all I know about the professor is that his name is Daniel, and this is his blogpost: https://turingmachine.org This video comes out of this livestream: https://youtube.com/live/hsPHmIMURqw I know, audio is not leveled but we had technical difficulties the day of the recording and not much I can or want to do about it at this point. Monthly call with the YouTube members that have the "CEO" level in which we'll discuss several interesting topics. This is transmitted live with everyone, so you can join and chat with us during the call What is this podcast about, why, and where to find it? https://linkarzu.com/about/#whats-the-linkarzu-ceos-monthly-hangout-podcast If you're not a YouTube member yet, you can join here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCrSIvbFncPSlK6AdwE2QboA/join

item.title: Doom Emacs: Why I Chose It Over Vim and Never Looked Back
item.description:
youtube

Some of you may know Emacs as the rival to Vim, and we know that Vim has been increasing popularity. However, I like to go against the grain, which means I pivoted to Doom Emacs a couple years back, and I haven't looked back. In this video, I’m diving deep into why Doom Emacs has become my go-to choice for coding and productivity. If you’re tired of your current setup or just curious about what Doom Emacs can offer, you’re in the right place! Doom Emacs is not just another text editor; it’s a powerful framework that enhances the Emacs experience. With its keyboard-centric workflow, you can do everything from coding to managing tasks, all in one place. I’ll share my favourite packages, explore some underutilized features, and even show you how it integrates seamlessly with my Linux distro, Nix. One of the standout features for me is org-mode, which has transformed how I manage tasks and notes. I’ll walk you through my simplistic workflow and how you can easily adopt it to boost your productivity. Plus, I’ll discuss the benefits of using open-source software and how Doom Emacs supports a variety of programming languages without the hassle of extensive customization. If you’ve ever felt overwhelmed by the complexity of Emacs, don’t worry! I’ll explain how Doom Emacs simplifies the experience while still allowing for customization. You’ll learn how to become a power user without spending hours reading documentation. And for those of you who have experience with Vim, you’ll be pleased to know that your skills transfer over seamlessly with Doom Emacs’s evil mode. Join me as I explore the incredible capabilities of Doom Emacs, from its intuitive key bindings to its stability and performance. Whether you’re a seasoned developer or just starting out, this video will provide valuable insights into how you can enhance your coding experience. Don’t forget to like and subscribe for more content on Emacs and coding productivity! ⟡˖ Links https://magit.vc/ https://pandoc.org/ https://www.reddit.com/r/emacs/comments/1gybfgm/why_use_magit/ emacs overlay - https://github.com/bashbunni/dotfiles/blob/main/configuration.nix#L147-L151 another great video about org mode - https://youtu.be/0-brF21ShRk ⟡˖ My other corners of the Internet: https://twitch.tv/bashbunni https://github.com/bashbunni https://discord.com/invite/Y3vbKS4K9j (come hang!) https://mastodon.social/@bashbunni https://twitter.com/sudobunni https://bsky.app/profile/bashbunni.dev Chapters: 00:00:00 - From Vim to Doom Emacs 00:00:33 - Emacs: More than a Text Editor 00:01:17 - The Jump to Org-mode 00:01:57 - Vim Keybindings in Emacs 00:02:35 - Productivity Out of the Box 00:03:53 - Prioritizing Stability 00:05:05 - Framework vs. Custom Configuration 00:06:08 - Managing Packages with Nix Overlays 00:07:26 - Magit: Visibility and Control 00:09:40 - Local PR Reviews with Magit 00:11:43 - Magit vs. Traditional Git GUIs 00:15:56 - Interactive Rebasing & Discoverability 00:17:55 - Vterm & Bookmark Workflows 00:18:51 - Exporting Org-mode to Any Format 00:22:02 - Effortless Task Management 00:24:06 - Personal Monthly Workflow 00:25:29 - Future Exploration & Outro Let’s get started and unlock the full potential of Doom Emacs together! What if you used emacs? Yet another emacs video something about emacs my hands never leave the keyboard meet your doom (emacs) Are You Making a Mistake by Not Using Doom Emacs? Unlock Your Coding Potential with Doom Emacs Doom Emacs: Why I Chose It Over Vim and Never Looked Back ,#bashbunni #programming #linux #emacs

item.title: Xah Talk Show Ep796. emacs lisp. add width height to image thumbnails html. part 3
item.description:
youtube

Video Summary (Generated by AI, Edited by Human.) In this episode (Ep796), Xah Lee spends the livestream coding an Emacs Lisp script to automatically add width and height attributes to image thumbnail HTML markup across his vast website, which contains over 13,000 pages (0:03-2:38). Key activities and topics covered: • Web Development and Emacs: Xah demonstrates the power of his custom Emacs setup, including his Xah Fly Keys system, to efficiently process files, convert images to WebP format (19:51), and perform bulk updates on his HTML files (1:50:23). • Technical Workflow: He walks through writing and testing an Emacs Lisp script that uses regular expressions to find image tags missing dimension attributes and insert them dynamically (56:12 - 1:22:38). • Reflections on Tools: Throughout the stream, Xah shares his strong opinions on various tech topics, including: • Comparing his workflow to "typical Unix idiots" who rely on manual bash commands (18:40). • His preference for the Wolfram Language (Mathematica) as the most powerful programming language (22:46). • His experience with image viewers like ImageGlass (11:34). • Project Progress: By the end of the stream, he successfully tests his script on local files (1:46:42-1:50:04), refines it to handle multiple occurrences in a single file using a while loop (1:50:45), and explains how he would scale this to process his entire website (1:54:47-2:00:48). • The stream is informal, featuring Xah sharing personal anecdotes about his life and coding history while enjoying red wine (0:26, 55:51). Notes at http://xahlee.info/talk_show/xah_talk_show_ep796.html If browser says security error, that is because it auto changes URL from HTTP to HTTPS. It's a way to censor 22 million old websites. Remove the S in HTTPS. Or use brave browser or safari.

item.title: Construindo um pacote do Emacs como um asteca
item.description:
youtube

Vou escrever uma coisa útil para mim sem o uso de agentes de IA e lendo documentação. No caso, eu passei um tempinho configurando um pacote para dbt no emacs.

item.title: Speech to text with Whisper-cpp Emacs and whisper.el
item.description:
youtube

Speech to text with Whisper-cpp built with Vulkan support to use the GPU and Emacs with Sacha Chua's fork of the whisper.el package with additional features The Emacs whisper.el package and whisper.cpp work on Linux, Mac, Windows and Freebsd Using a keyboard shortcut in Emacs you can start recording with your microphone, and then press the same keyboard when you have finished to insert the transcribed text into the buffer. In this video i cover how to use you own build of Whisper-cpp with Vulkan support to use the GPU, how to specify a model to use and on Freebsd use oss audio and specify the microphone. Links: Whisper-Cpp build with Vulkan https://github.com/NapoleonWils0n/cerberus/blob/master/whisper-cpp/whisper-cpp-vulkan-freebsd.org Emacs init.el whisper.el https://github.com/NapoleonWils0n/freebsd-dotfiles-xps/blob/master/.config/emacs/init.el#L1613 whisper.cpp https://github.com/ggml-org/whisper.cpp Sacha Chua's fork of whisper.el https://github.com/sachac/whisper.el Sacha Chua's blog post https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/01/using-whisper-el-to-capture-text-to-speech-in-emacs/ Yay Emacs live-coding: whisper.el https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LtfxMFW0574 Sacha Chua's dotfiles https://sachachua.com/dotemacs/index.html#multimedia-whisper https://github.com/sachac/.emacs.d Freebsd dotfiles https://github.com/NapoleonWils0n/freebsd-dotfiles-xps Freebsd root dotfile https://github.com/NapoleonWils0n/freebsd-root-xps

item.title: How to Split the Screen in Emacs
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to split the screen in Emacs. I first show you how to create a horizontal split with the command: C-x 2 I then show you how to split the screen vertically with the command: C-x 3 I then show you how to navigate between windows with the command: C-x o I show you how to close all other files other than the one you're currently focused on with: C-x 1 And I show you how to close all the file you're currently focused on with: C-x 0 I hope this helps, enjoy.

item.title: Emacs HideShow Minor Mode For Folding And Unfolding 2026_06_21_03:01:51
item.description:
youtube

In this video, you will see,Emacs HideShow minor mode capability. ,#linux #linuxadmin #sysadmin #opensource #Gentoo #Debian #Slackware #research ,#linuxkernel #kernel #gnu #Linux #GNU Buy Me A Coffee: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/unixbhaskar Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/unixbhaskar Paypal: https://paypal.me/linuxbhaskar You can also find me : GitHub: https://github.com/unixbhaskar Reddit: https://www.reddit.com/user/unixbhaskar Blogs: https://unixbhaskar.wordpress.com Twitter: https://twitter.com/unixbhaskar Linkedin: https://linkedin.com/in/unixbhaskar Vimeo: https://vimeo.com/unixbhaskar Goodreads: https://goodreads.com/unixbhaskar

item.title: Xah Talk Show Ep795. emacs lisp. add width height to html image thumbnails. part 2
item.description:
youtube

Video Summary (Generated by AI, Edited by Human.) In this technical coding session, Xah Lee demonstrates how he updates his massive collection of approximately 10,000 HTML files to include width and height attributes for image thumbnails. This process is intended to prevent cumulative layout shift (CLS), improving webpage stability and performance (0:00-1:55). Key highlights from the session include: • Coding Process: Xah works on updating his icon-linkify Emacs Lisp command (7:12). • He shows several emacs wrappers for commands that get image dimensions across different operating systems—specifically comparing macOS (sips command), Linux (file utility), Windows (PowerShell), and ImageMagick (20:18-28:00). • emacs bug: He tells the story of emacs bug when calculating image sizes natively for certain formats like PNGs (23:36) and discusses the potential for using Python or Go to handle these calculations more reliably (27:15). • Automation: To process his thousands of files, he experiments with keyboard macros in Emacs (43:07) but ultimately concludes that a custom Emacs Lisp script is necessary to automate the bulk update efficiently (56:17). • Rants and Tangents: Throughout the session, he critiques the bloat of modern software tools like ImageMagick (21:28) and takes time to explain computer architecture, specifically the speed differences between L1 cache, RAM, and flash storage/spinning disks (57:27-1:01:36). Notes at http://xahlee.info/talk_show/xah_talk_show_ep795.html If browser says security error, that is because it auto changes URL from HTTP to HTTPS. It's a way to censor 22 million old websites. Remove the S in HTTPS. Or use brave browser or safari.

item.title: Elfeed 4 0 0 使用分享
item.description:
youtube

Elfeed 是 Emacs 中的 RSS 閱讀器，視頻分享了 Elfeed 4.0.0 的使用。 文章鏈接: taxodium.ink/elfeed-4-0-0-usage-sharing.html (請自行補全 https:// , 因為我目前無法在描述串加鏈接)

item.title: Emacs Chat 26: Ross A. Baker
item.description:
youtube

https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/06/emacs-chat-with-ross-a-baker/ Related links: - Blog https://rossabaker.com/blog/ - Posts tagged #emacs https://rossabaker.com/tags/emacs/ - Ross A. Baker: Take Two: Coming Home to Emacs https://rossabaker.com/blog/take-two-coming-home-to-emacs/ - Emacs config https://rossabaker.com/configs/emacs/ - Emacs Early Init https://rossabaker.com/configs/emacs/early-init/ - Khlav Kalash Emacs https://rossabaker.com/configs/emacs/khlav-kalash/ - Crab Juice Emacs https://rossabaker.com/configs/emacs/crab-juice/ - Starter Emacs configuration https://rossabaker.com/configs/emacs/rab-starter/ - matthewbauer/bauer: An Emacs+Nix IDE · GitHub https://github.com/matthewbauer/bauer - Ross A. Baker: Multilingualism in a Global Web https://rossabaker.com/blog/multilingualism_in_a_global_web/ (DE https://rossabaker.com/blog/multilingualism_in_a_global_web/, ES https://es.rossabaker.com/blog/multiling%C3%BCismo_en_una_web_global/) 00:00 Opening 00:46 What Ross does 02:06 How Ross got into Emacs, used other editors, and then came back to Emacs 04:58 Config focusing on built-ins 10:12 simple-orderless 14:29 Size indicator 16:40 Graceful degradation 17:48 emacs-lock-mode 19:52 exiting Emacs: yes-or-no-p 20:46 yes-or-no-p 21:45 Processes 22:18 Moving keymaps 24:23 Writing in Org Mode and Markdown 27:49 Ethersync? 29:16 Managing Github with Forge 35:49 Committing with work addresses vs personal 37:13 Emacs tinkering as stress relief 41:06 Under-appreciated Emacs built-ins 42:20 gptel 46:04 Getting older 46:46 Lindy's Law and tool longevity 49:51 Nix is good at managing package versions and customizing them; Matthew Bauer (Bauer IDE) 53:25 Custom fonts 54:30 Starter kits versus configs 55:34 Nix vs Guix; Mac 56:26 Non-work interests: Org for documenting; ox-hugo and multiple languages Find more Emacs Chats or join the fun: https://sachachua.com/emacs-chat

item.title: Emacs Teaches Emacs: The Missing README
item.description:
youtube

This is the companion post for my blog post on how Emacs teaches itself through its rich auto-discovery (or help) system. This is a great tutorial for beginners, as it teaches you how to teach yourself! Blog post: https://www.chiply.dev/post-ask-emacs Longer version: https://www.chiply.dev/post-june-emacs-carnival Timestamps: 0:00 Intro 1:24 M-x 2:47 Help Commands 4:30 The Prefix Trick 7:18 which-key 9:38 C-h C-h 10:50 Outro ,#emacs #textediting #developertools #software #programming #tutorial

item.title: Bending Emacs Episode 14: Prototyping iOS apps with agent-shell, artist-mode, and Claude Skills
item.description: youtube

item.title: Juneau Something? Emacs Teaches You How to Fish!
item.description:
youtube

You don't learn Emacs so much as you learn the handful of built-in commands for asking Emacs about Emacs — and those same commands work on themselves, so once you have them you can teach yourself everything else. This post tours that machinery (the C-h help family, apropos, Info, xref, describe- and friends) by following the questions a curious beginner actually asks, and ends by fishing a built-in game out of Emacs and reading its source with the very tools we just learned. It's the fish that teaches fishing 🎣. Blog post: https://www.chiply.dev/post-june-emacs-carnival Timestamps 0:00 Intro 5:45 help-for-help 9:19 help-with-tutorial 10:35 info-emacs-manual 14:12 view-lossage 16:30 repeat-complex-command 19:08 view-echo-area-messages 21:05 eldoc-mode 23:38 display-local-help 24:37 what-cursor-position 26:30 describe-mode 29:50 describe-bindings 30:55 where-is 32:00 which-key-mode 34:30 execute-extended-command 35:35 tmm-menubar 37:04 describe-[thing] 39:35 apropos 43:35 Info 48:25 finder-by-keyword 49:09 view-emacs-news 49:47 xref 53:10 find-[thing] 55:25 eval and ielm 59:38 customize 1:06:14 tetris! 1:12:08 Outro ,#emacs #tutorial #software #programming #developertools #textediting

item.title: Emacs Org-Roam: Gestione della Conoscenza a Grafi e Metodo Zettelkasten
item.description:
youtube

Abbandonare la rigida struttura gerarchica a cartelle e sottocartelle è il primo passo per costruire un sistema di gestione della conoscenza personale (PKM) davvero efficiente. In questo video esploriamo come trasformare Emacs in un potente "secondo cervello" non gerarchico attraverso Org-Roam, un'estensione ispirata ai principi metodologici dello Zettelkasten. Analizzeremo la teoria dei grafi applicata alle tue note, esaminando come l'estensione traduca i concetti di nodi e collegamenti bidirezionali (backlinks) in metadati nativi di Org-mode, senza ricorrere a formati proprietari ma sfruttando la persistenza degli ID alfanumerici registrati su database SQLite. -- Contatti -- Canale telegram: https://t.me/NFVblog Pagina ko-fi per le donazioni: https://ko-fi.com/nfvblog Pagina Mastodon: https://mastodon.cloud/@nfvblog

item.title: Xah Talk Show Ep793. emacs, install Xah Fly Keys in WSL linux emacs
item.description:
youtube

Video Summary (Generated by AI, Edited by Human.) In this video, Xah Lee demonstrates the process of installing his custom Emacs configuration, specifically Xah Fly Keys, within a Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) environment. The session is highly technical and covers his troubleshooting process for setting up a functional development environment in Linux. Key Highlights: • Goal and Motivation: The primary objective is to migrate his established Emacs workflow from Windows to WSL, ensuring he can use his custom keybindings and packages effectively (0:17-1:24). • Technical Challenges: A significant portion of the video details the hurdles encountered, such as: • Font Configuration: Debugging why font sizes and families (specifically Deja Vu Sans Mono) were not applying correctly in the Linux environment (51:38, 57:26, 1:04:19). • File Management: Navigating the Linux filesystem and managing init files compared to his usual Windows setup (1:30:03-1:49:45). • Performance: Comparing the startup speed of Emacs on Windows versus WSL (1:55:07-1:56:15). • Philosophy and Commentary: Xah Lee provides commentary throughout on his distaste for certain Unix conventions, particularly the historic 80-character line-length limitation, which he argues causes unnecessary formatting issues for modern users (2:07:31-2:12:35). Conclusion: By the end of the video, Xah Lee successfully got Xah Fly Keys set his Emacs setup on WSL. also mentioned is that there seems to be missing native code compilation on the WSL instance. (2:16:49-2:17:58). Notes at http://xahlee.info/talk_show/xah_talk_show_ep793.html If browser says security error, that is because it auto changes URL from HTTP to HTTPS. It's a way to censor 22 million old websites. Remove the S in HTTPS. Or use brave browser or safari.

item.title: Vibe Coding with Emacs' Built-In AI - System Crafters Live!
item.description:
youtube

In this stream, we'll finally give vibe coding a try! But forget the big-name cloud assistants: it turns out Emacs has been shipping with its own AI assistant for decades, so we'll be pair programming with that instead. The rules are simple: we start a fresh project, we consult our assistant before every decision, and whatever it says, goes. Let's see if we can learn what all the vibe coding fuss is about! ,#emacs #vibecoding #aicoding #pairprogramming #lisp SUPPORT THE CHANNEL: 👍 Support My Work: https://systemcrafters.net/how-to-help/#support-my-work 📰 Subscribe to the Newsletter: https://systemcrafters.net/newsletter/ 👕 Check out the Store: https://systemcrafters.store 📘 Get Your Copy of Mastering Emacs: https://www.masteringemacs.org/r/systemcrafters?utm_source=yt&utm_medium=desc&utm_campaign=scme SHOW NOTES: https://systemcrafters.net/live-streams/june-12-2026/ JOIN THE COMMUNITY: https://systemcrafters.net/community/ (Forum and IRC chat!) https://fosstodon.org/@daviwil MY CONFIGURATION: (This site is currently down but will be back up soon!) https://config.daviwil.com https://config.daviwil.com/emacs https://config.daviwil.com/systems (Guix) OTHER SERIES: - Emacs Essentials: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=48JlgiBpw_I&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oPZvSdewHG8uApD7THlLLCV - Emacs From Scratch: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=74zOY-vgkyw&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oPH1au7H6B7bBJ4ZO7BXjSZ - Emacs Tips: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wKTKmE1wLyw&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oMHJ6Xil1YdnYtlWd5hHZql - Emacs Desktop Environment: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f7xB2fFk1tQ&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oNPbEMYEtswOVTvq7CVddCS - Emacs IDE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E-NAM9U5JYE&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oNvsrtk_iZSb94krGRofFjN - Emacs Mail: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yZRyEhi4y44&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oM-kA19xOQc8s0gr0PpFGJQ - Learning Emacs Lisp: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RQK_DaaX34Q&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oPQtn7FQEF3D7sroZbXuPZ7 - Craft Your System with GNU Guix: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iBaqOK75cho&list=PLEoMzSkcN8oNxnj7jm5V2ZcGc52002pQU CREDITS: Coriolis Effect by logos feat. stefsax, licensed Creative Commons 3.0 CC-BY http://ccmixter.org/files/mseq/26296 reNovation by airtone, licensed Creative Commons 3.0 CC-BY http://ccmixter.org/files/airtone/60674 ukeSounds by airtone, licensed Creative Commons 3.0 CC-BY http://ccmixter.org/files/airtone/32655 Between Worlds (Instrumental) by Aussens@iter, licensed Creative Commons 3.0 CC-BY http://ccmixter.org/files/tobias_weber/56664

item.title: Yay Emacs 33: Sacha and Prot Talk Emacs: Built-ins
item.description:
youtube

https://sachachua.com/blog/2026/06/june-11-sacha-and-prot-talk-emacs-built-ins/ On June 11, I'm going to chat with Prot https://protesilaos.com about the Emacs Carnival June 2026 topic Underappreciated Emacs Built-ins https://rossabaker.com/blog/underappreciated-emacs-built-ins/. Thanks to Ross A. Baker for hosting the carnival! 2026-06-11 Thu 10:30--2026-06-11 Thu 11:30 (America/Toronto) = Thu Jun 11 1030H EDT / 0930H CDT / 0830H MDT / 0730H PDT / 1430H UTC / 1630H CEST / 1730H EEST / 2000H IST / 2230H +08 / 2330H JST

item.title: Xah Talk Show Ep792. install emacs on wsl Windows Subsystem for Linux
item.description:
youtube

In this episode of Xah Talk Show, the creator walks viewers through the process of installing and compiling Emacs on the Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL). The session is informal, characterized by the creator's commentary on technology, his views on software development history, and his personal anecdotes. Key sections of the video include: Introduction to WSL: The creator explains how to enable the necessary virtualization settings in the BIOS/UEFI and the basic commands to initialize WSL on Windows (0:00 - 9:00). The Installation Process: Throughout the video, the creator struggles with and successfully navigates installing dependencies (such as build-essential, libxpm, libgtk, etc.) required to build Emacs from source (13:30 - 1:18:20). Historical Commentary: The creator offers his perspectives on the history of package managers (e.g., Think, MacPorts, Homebrew, dpkg), the evolution of open-source software, and the influence of corporations like Microsoft and Google (51:45 - 55:40). The Ubuntu Logo Story: A notable tangent where the creator discusses the origin and perceived ideological implications of the Ubuntu logo (36:34 - 41:43). Successful Compilation and Demo: After a long build process, the creator successfully runs the Emacs binary (version 32) within WSL and performs a quick demo of the Tetris game built into Emacs to verify the installation (1:44:00 - 1:46:39). Notes at http://xahlee.info/talk_show/xah_talk_show_ep792.html If browser says security error, that is because it auto changes URL from HTTP to HTTPS. It's a way to censor 22 million old websites. Remove the S in HTTPS. Or use brave browser or safari.

item.title: Emacs - world-cup.el: Gettin' hyped for the 2026 World Cup
item.description:
youtube

I pretty much only get into soccer for the world cup every four years -- at which point, I have a lot of studying to do! I'd way rather do that in emacs than anywhere else. So I vibecoded world-cup.el -- and here's me showing it off. Source Repo: https://github.com/strickinato/org-world-cup-2026/ Project that inspired: https://www.sastibe.de/2018/06/its-here-org-agenda-for-the-world-cup-2018/ Don't forget, touch grass...

item.title: How to Install a Package on Emacs From Melpa
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to install a custom package from the Melpa package repository by first enabling packages at startup and then how, without elisp, to add the Melpa package repository. I then show you how to install the theme "Atom One Dark" and also a cool website to use to find themes.

item.title: How I structure my Emacs init.el file
item.description:
youtube

I cover how my init.el file is structured. You can find it on my github: https://github.com/maxfriis/my-emacs-config Note that I forgot to mention, why I don't use use-package or straight. The reason is that I don't want to micromanage what get deferred. I trust that the packages I use defer sensibly. Furthermore if you want to utilize use-package, I feel it invites you to have a more messy structure. E.g. I like that all my keybindings are in a separate section.

item.title: How to Change Themes in Emacs
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to change from the default theme of Emacs into another one of the themes that comes with Emacs by using the command: M-x customize-themes I then show you how to change to that theme and save that theme preference for future sessions when using Emacs.

item.title: Corrigindo divergências entre arquivos de configuração do emacs
item.description:
youtube

Me veja tentando resolver divergências acumuladas nos últimos 6 meses.

item.title: How to Inhibit the Welcome Screen in Emacs
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to inhibit the welcome screen in Emacs so that you can start the editor with a blank buffer. To do this, I have you customize the option (with M-x customize-option) "inhibit-startup-screen". You'll want to toggle that to on and then click "apply and save" within the editor. After that, when you restart the editor it will start with a blank buffer.

item.title: How to Maximize Emacs' Screen Upon Startup
item.description:
youtube

In this video, I show you how to maximize the Emacs editor upon startup with the following commands: M-x "customize-option" This first command allows you to customize a variable. The variable you want to customize is "default-frame-alist". With this variable you want to create a parameter called "fullscreen" and give it the value "maximized". You'll then click apply and save and the next time you startup Emacs it will maximize to the full screen size.
